req.title: Feature flag rollout
req.statement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment; toggles shall be audit-logged.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-109 governs feature-flag toggling restricted to admin roles with mandatory audit logging. Four test cases are derived: (1) EP positive – admin successfully toggles a flag and an audit entry is created; (2) NEG negative – non-admin user receives HTTP 403; (3) STC boundary – flag toggled from enabled→disabled→enabled verifying state machine correctness and two audit entries; (4) DT decision-table – simultaneous toggle across two environments to confirm per-environment isolation. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create/clean up flag state.

tests.name: test_admin_can_toggle_feature_flag_and_audit_logged
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:admin
Feature flag 'BETA_DASHBOARD' exists in environment 'staging' with state disabled
actions: Locate the toggle for 'BETA_DASHBOARD' in the 'staging' row
Click the toggle to enable the flag
Query the audit log API for the most recent entry for 'BETA_DASHBOARD'
expected: Flag 'BETA_DASHBOARD' state changes to enabled on the UI
Audit log contains an entry with actor=admin_user, flag=BETA_DASHBOARD, env=staging, new_state=enabled
trace: R-WEB-109: audit entry created on toggle
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_can_toggle_feature_flag_and_audit_logged(
    admin_page,          # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for admin
    ensure_flag_state,   # <fixture>: sets flag to given state pre-test
    audit_log_client,    # <fixture>: thin API wrapper for audit-log queries
):
    ensure_flag_state(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging", state="disabled")
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/feature-flags")
    toggle = page.locator(
        "[data-testid='flag-row-BETA_DASHBOARD-staging'] [data-testid='toggle']"
    )
    expect(toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "false")
    toggle.click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    expect(toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "true")
    entry = audit_log_client.latest_entry(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging")
    assert entry["new_state"] == "enabled"
    assert entry["actor_role"] == "admin"
    assert entry["environment"] == "staging"

tests.name: test_non_admin_toggle_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:viewer (not admin)
actions: Send a direct PATCH request to /feature-flags/BETA_DASHBOARD with env=staging, state=enabled using the viewer's auth token
expected: Response body contains error code 'FORBIDDEN' or message indicating insufficient privileges
No audit entry is created for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-109: error pathway – 403 when not admin
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_toggle_returns_403(
    viewer_api_client,   # <fixture>: requests.Session authenticated as role:viewer
    ensure_flag_state,   # <fixture>
    audit_log_client,    # <fixture>
):
    ensure_flag_state(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging", state="disabled")
    before_count = audit_log_client.entry_count(
        flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging"
    )
    resp = viewer_api_client.patch(
        "/feature-flags/BETA_DASHBOARD",
        json={"environment": "staging", "state": "enabled"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") in ("FORBIDDEN", "Forbidden") or \
           "permission" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    after_count = audit_log_client.entry_count(
        flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging"
    )
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit entry should be written on 403"

tests.name: test_flag_toggle_state_cycle_enabled_disabled_enabled
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:admin
Feature flag 'BETA_DASHBOARD' exists in environment 'staging' with state enabled
actions: Click toggle for 'BETA_DASHBOARD' in 'staging' to disable it (enabled→disabled)
Verify UI shows disabled and audit entry #1 recorded
Click toggle again to re-enable it (disabled→enabled)
Verify UI shows enabled and audit entry #2 recorded
expected: After first toggle: flag state is disabled; audit entry new_state=disabled
After second toggle: flag state is enabled; audit entry new_state=enabled
Exactly 2 new audit entries are created during this test
trace: R-WEB-109: audit entry created on each toggle (state transition coverage)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_flag_toggle_state_cycle_enabled_disabled_enabled(
    admin_page,
    ensure_flag_state,
    audit_log_client,
):
    ensure_flag_state(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging", state="enabled")
    baseline = audit_log_client.entry_count(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging")
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/feature-flags")
    toggle = page.locator(
        "[data-testid='flag-row-BETA_DASHBOARD-staging'] [data-testid='toggle']"
    )
    # Transition 1: enabled -> disabled
    expect(toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "true")
    toggle.click()
    expect(toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "false")
    e1 = audit_log_client.latest_entry(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging")
    assert e1["new_state"] == "disabled"
    # Transition 2: disabled -> enabled
    toggle.click()
    expect(toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "true")
    e2 = audit_log_client.latest_entry(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging")
    assert e2["new_state"] == "enabled"
    final_count = audit_log_client.entry_count(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging")
    assert final_count - baseline == 2

tests.name: test_flag_toggle_is_isolated_per_environment
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:admin
Feature flag 'BETA_DASHBOARD' exists in both 'staging' and 'production' with state disabled
actions: Read the flag state for 'staging' and 'production' from the UI
Query the audit log for both environments
expected: Audit log has exactly 1 new entry for staging with new_state=enabled
Audit log has 0 new entries for production
trace: R-WEB-109: toggles shall be per environment
R-WEB-109: audit entry created only for toggled environment
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_flag_toggle_is_isolated_per_environment(
    admin_page,
    ensure_flag_state,
    audit_log_client,
):
    ensure_flag_state(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging", state="disabled")
    ensure_flag_state(flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="production", state="disabled")
    prod_baseline = audit_log_client.entry_count(
        flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="production"
    )
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/feature-flags")
    staging_toggle = page.locator(
        "[data-testid='flag-row-BETA_DASHBOARD-staging'] [data-testid='toggle']"
    )
    prod_toggle = page.locator(
        "[data-testid='flag-row-BETA_DASHBOARD-production'] [data-testid='toggle']"
    )
    staging_toggle.click()
    expect(staging_toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "true")
    expect(prod_toggle).to_have_attribute("aria-checked", "false")
    staging_entry = audit_log_client.latest_entry(
        flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="staging"
    )
    assert staging_entry["new_state"] == "enabled"
    prod_count = audit_log_client.entry_count(
        flag="BETA_DASHBOARD", env="production"
    )
    assert prod_count == prod_baseline
